name: Paper To Implementation Plan
description: Turn a paper or method note into a scoped, evidence-backed implementation plan without launching runtime work.

inputs.description: Paper URL, arXiv identifier, Hugging Face Papers link, or method note.
Architecture, loss, data pipeline, evaluation method, or other component to implement.
Preferred implementation framework or repository stack.

permissions.description: Read public papers, linked repositories, model cards, dataset cards, and documentation.

approval_points.description: Confirm the selected component, assumptions, and non-goals before implementation work starts.

phases.name: Scope
phases.objective: Define implementation target, excluded paper claims, repository constraints, and acceptance criteria.

phases.name: Paper Extraction
phases.objective: Extract equations, architecture blocks, data assumptions, metrics, and cited implementation details.

phases.name: Interface Plan
phases.objective: Define public inputs, outputs, tensor shapes, configuration knobs, and integration boundaries.

phases.name: Delivery Plan
phases.objective: Produce an ordered implementation checklist with tests, evidence expectations, and unresolved risks.

required_outputs.description: Target component, constraints, assumptions, non-goals, and acceptance criteria.
Paper method extraction with source citations.
Paper claims mapped to sections, figures, tables, and code references.
Inputs, outputs, configuration, and repository integration points.
Tensor shape expectations and module boundary assumptions.
Ordered implementation tasks, tests, evidence expectations, and risks.

artifacts.description: Actionable checklist for the future implementation slice.
Paper sections, external references, and planned code touchpoints.

verifiers.description: Implementation target, non-goals, and acceptance criteria are explicit.
Implementation requirements cite paper sections, figures, tables, repositories, or cards.
Inputs, outputs, dtypes, configuration, and repository boundaries are explicit.
Shape contract covers planned public module boundaries.
Final plan claims cite method notes, claim table, interface contract, and risk notes.
